name: Hurakan
description: Hurakan, also known as Hurrican, is a powerful creator god in Mayan mythology, particularly prominent in the Popol Vuh, the sacred book of the K'iche' Maya. He is one of the creator deities who participated in the various attempts to create humankind. Hurakan is often associated with storms, wind, fire, and tempests. He is considered a sky god and a divine wind, embodying the destructive and creative forces of nature. Hurakan played a crucial role in the creation myths, unleashing floods to destroy the previous, imperfect versions of humanity made of wood. His name literally translates to "one-legged" or "one-legged god," although the precise meaning and significance of this epithet remain debated among scholars. He is described as a heart of sky, a powerful force that shapes and reshapes the world. While destructive, his actions are ultimately aimed at paving the way for a more perfect creation. He is a member of the creator triad alongside Kukulkan (feathered serpent) and Tepeu, and together they planned and executed the creation of the world and its inhabitants. Hurakan's power is immense, capable of both devastation and renewal, making him a central figure in Mayan cosmology and religious beliefs. He commands the elements and serves as a constant reminder of the awesome power of the natural world and the cyclical nature of creation and destruction.
subtitle: Heart of Sky, Wind, and Storm

keyMyths.title: The Great Flood
keyMyths.description: Hurakan unleashed a great flood to destroy the wooden people, deemed unworthy of existence by the creator gods. This flood served as a cleansing, preparing the world for a new creation.

keyMyths.title: Creation of Humanity
keyMyths.description: Hurakan, along with Kukulkan and Tepeu, participated in the attempts to create humans, ultimately succeeding with humans made of maize. He is a vital part of the creative process.

keyMyths.title: Hurakan's Role in the Cosmos
keyMyths.description: As the heart of sky, Hurakan is a fundamental force in the Mayan cosmos, responsible for maintaining balance and order. He embodies the power of the sky and its influence on the earth.
